machinery_genes.role: accessory inner-membrane assembly factor (ExeAB complex; PG-binding ATPase, drives secretin assembly)
machinery_genes.quote: Analysis of this fragment revealed an operon-like structure with two complete genes, exeA and exeB
machinery_genes.note: Jahagirdar & Howard 1994 isolated the second (exeAB / gspAB) operon of the A. hydrophila T2SS. ExeA is a hydrophilic 60-kDa protein with a consensus ATP-binding site; the ExeAB complex binds peptidoglycan and is required to assemble the ExeD secretin (later work: PMID 20345654, 20971903). Accessory GspA family per the brief, not part of the exeC-N pseudopilus operon.

machinery_genes.role: accessory inner-membrane assembly factor (ExeAB complex; binds the secretin ExeD)
machinery_genes.quote: Complementation required the presence of both exeA and exeB, and marker exchange mutagenesis confirmed the requirement for both gene products for secretion.
machinery_genes.note: Jahagirdar & Howard 1994. ExeB is a 25-kDa basic protein with limited homology to PulB (Klebsiella) and OutB (Erwinia); inner-membrane-anchored partner of ExeA. Periplasmic domain of ExeB directly interacts with ExeD secretin (PMID 25025769). Accessory GspB family.

machinery_genes.role: inner-membrane platform component (links IM platform to secretin)
machinery_genes.quote: involving the products of at least two operons, exeAB (gspAB) and exeC-N (gspC-N)
machinery_genes.note: ExeC is the first gene of the exeC-N (gspC-N) operon, named here by Ast et al 2002 with explicit gsp equivalence (gspC-N). No individual functional-characterization quote for ExeC was found; role assigned from the GspC inner-membrane platform family in the apparatus brief. Operon-membership call, moderate confidence.

machinery_genes.role: outer-membrane secretin (the secretion port / multimeric channel)
machinery_genes.quote: the localization and assembly of the ExeD secretion port multimer
machinery_genes.note: ExeD is the A. hydrophila T2SS secretin (pentadecameric outer-membrane ring; cryo-EM structure in PMID 31083688). Its outer-membrane assembly depends on the ExeAB complex. Best-characterized component of this system. GspD family.

machinery_genes.quote: It contains two complete genes, exeE and exeF, plus fragments of two others and may form part of an operon.
machinery_genes.note: Jiang & Howard 1992, the founding paper of the A. hydrophila general secretion pathway. ExeE is a 501-residue protein required for secretion and outer-membrane assembly; it is the GspE-family secretion ATPase (the gene whose Tn5 insertion mutant L1.97 defined the system). Named member of the exeC-N operon.

machinery_genes.quote: It contains two complete genes, exeE and exeF, plus fragments of two others and may form part of an operon.
machinery_genes.note: Jiang & Howard 1992; ExeF is a 388-residue protein co-encoded with ExeE. GspF-family integral IM platform component. ExeE and ExeF are 'highly similar to other proteins which have been shown to be involved in extracellular secretion'.

machinery_genes.quote: Limited processing of the ExeG protein was observed when it was expressed in Escherichia coli, and this processing was greatly accelerated in the presence of the prepilin peptidase of Pseudomonas aeruginosa.
machinery_genes.note: Howard, Critch & Bedi 1993 sequenced exeG-exeN. ExeG carries a type IV prepilin signal and is processed by prepilin peptidase, identifying it as the GspG-family major pseudopilin of the Exe pseudopilus.

machinery_genes.quote: we have isolated and sequenced a further eight exe genes, exeG through exeN, which constitute the 3' end of the exe operon
machinery_genes.note: Howard et al 1993. ExeH is one of the eight exeG-exeN genes sequenced as the 3' end of the exe operon. Five of the Exe proteins carry type IV prepilin signal sequences; ExeH assigned to the GspH minor-pseudopilin family per the brief. Operon-membership call (no individual functional quote).

machinery_genes.quote: we have isolated and sequenced a further eight exe genes, exeG through exeN, which constitute the 3' end of the exe operon
machinery_genes.note: Howard et al 1993. exeI is within the exeG-exeN block. GspI minor-pseudopilin family per the brief. Operon-membership call.

machinery_genes.quote: we have isolated and sequenced a further eight exe genes, exeG through exeN, which constitute the 3' end of the exe operon
machinery_genes.note: Howard et al 1993. exeJ is within the exeG-exeN block. GspJ minor-pseudopilin family per the brief. Operon-membership call.

machinery_genes.quote: we have isolated and sequenced a further eight exe genes, exeG through exeN, which constitute the 3' end of the exe operon
machinery_genes.note: Howard et al 1993. exeK is within the exeG-exeN block. GspK minor-pseudopilin family per the brief. Operon-membership call.

machinery_genes.role: inner-membrane platform component (binds the ATPase ExeE)
machinery_genes.quote: we have isolated and sequenced a further eight exe genes, exeG through exeN, which constitute the 3' end of the exe operon
machinery_genes.note: Howard et al 1993. exeL is within the exeG-exeN block. GspL inner-membrane platform family per the brief. Operon-membership call.

machinery_genes.quote: we have isolated and sequenced a further eight exe genes, exeG through exeN, which constitute the 3' end of the exe operon
machinery_genes.note: Howard et al 1993. exeM is within the exeG-exeN block. GspM inner-membrane platform family per the brief. Operon-membership call.

machinery_genes.role: accessory inner-membrane component (terminal gene of the exeC-N operon)
machinery_genes.quote: we have isolated and sequenced a further eight exe genes, exeG through exeN, which constitute the 3' end of the exe operon
machinery_genes.note: Howard et al 1993; exeN is the terminal gene of the exeC-N operon (the 'N' boundary explicitly named in the quote). GspN accessory family per the brief. Operon-membership call, lowest confidence (no individual functional characterization), mirroring the EpsN treatment in the T2SS_01 pilot.

notes: The Exe system is the archetypal Aeromonas hydrophila T2SS characterized by S. Peter Howard and colleagues; it secretes aerolysin, amylase, lipase and protease (matching this instance's effectors aerA, amyA, lip, satA). The apparatus is encoded by TWO operons: exeAB (gspAB), the accessory inner-membrane assembly complex, and exeC-N (gspC-N), the core secretin + IM platform + pseudopilus genes (operon equivalence stated verbatim by Ast et al 2002, PMID 11967081). Founding/characterization papers all used A. hydrophila Ab449/C5.84/L1.97 derivatives, the same species as ATCC 7966, so no cross-strain transfer beyond strain background within the species. Coverage: secretin (ExeD), secretion ATPase (ExeE), IM platform (ExeC, ExeF, ExeL, ExeM), major + minor pseudopilins (ExeG-ExeK), accessory (ExeN), plus the GspA/GspB-family ExeAB assembly complex. ExeG individually shown to be a prepilin-peptidase-processed pseudopilin; ExeC and ExeH-ExeN are listed from operon-range naming quotes (exeC-N / exeG-exeN) with family roles from the apparatus brief, flagged as operon-membership calls (ExeN lowest confidence), the same standard as the EpsN call in the T2SS_01 pilot. Deliberately EXCLUDED: the prepilin peptidase (GspO/PilD/TapD) is required for pseudopilin maturation but Howard et al 1993 state 'the prepilin peptidase gene does not appear to form part of the exe operon'; no quote ties a specific A. hydrophila prepilin-peptidase gene NAME to this system, so it is not listed (would be a guess). Also excluded: secreted substrates (aerolysin etc.), Sec/Tat translocon, and regulators. All quotes are verbatim from the cited PubMed abstracts / Wiley title-abstract records; full-text bodies of these 1990s ASM/Wiley papers were not machine-retrievable, so per-gene homology detail relies on the operon-range and gsp-equivalence quotes rather than individual sequence sentences.
